location.office_time_required: occasional travel for project or team in-person meetings

level_signals: 5-8 years of experience in project or program management, customer success, or a similar role
Compensation range up to $171,900 base in highest-cost market

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or a related field; relevant experience may be considered.
5-8 years of experience in project or program management, customer success, or a similar role focused on partner engagement and program operations.
Strong project management skills, with the ability to develop and execute project plans, track milestones, and work cross-functionally.
Excellent communication skills for delivering complex information clearly across various partner communications, including newsletters, webinars, and surveys.
Experience with documentation management and content creation for program guides and enablement resources.
Demonstrated ability to drive process improvements and contribute to strategic initiatives within partner or program environments.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in SaaS or cloud-based technology environments.
Demonstrated understanding of partner programs and partner experience best practices.
Hands-on experience with partner or CRM technology stacks, specifically with tools like Impartner, MindTickle, Crossbeam and Salesforce (SFDC).
Proven expertise in partner onboarding, support management, and developing partner-focused workflows.
Familiarity with partner ecosystems, particularly working with Systems Integrators (SIs), ISVs, Resell, and Technology partners.
Experience in managing partner communications, tracking partner support tickets, and executing partner events.

explicitly_excluded: Not eligible to be hired in CA, CT, NJ, NY, PA, WA (for remote hiring purposes)
Compensation details only provided for CO, HI, IL, MD, MA, MN, VT, DC, NY, NJ, WA, CA

scope.surface: Partner ecosystem programs covering Systems Integrators (SIs), ISVs, Resell, and Technology partners
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / communications platform with partner channel
primary_metrics_inferred: Partner engagement (newsletter open rates, webinar attendance, survey response)
Support ticket resolution and escalation rates
core_responsibilities: Lead partner onboarding processes, optimize support workflows, and manage support ticket escalations
Serve as the primary point of contact for partner and internal inquiries related to Twilio's partner programs
Collaborate with marketing to drive engagement through partner events and certifications
Maintain and regularly update partner program documentation
Develop and manage partner communications, including monthly newsletters, quarterly product webinars, and feedback surveys
Contribute to strategic projects aimed at enhancing the partner experience and overall program impact
Identify areas for operational improvement and assist in implementing process enhancements
Develop and execute project plans for partner program initiatives
Track project progress, facilitate stakeholder updates, and ensure timely delivery of key milestones

compensation.equity_form: equity plan and corporate bonus plan

green_flags: Compensation ranges disclosed across multiple geographies
Application deadline stated (July 24, 2026), signaling active and structured hiring
Role scope is clearly defined with specific partner types (SIs, ISVs, Resell, Technology)
Equity and bonus participation mentioned in addition to base
Encourages non-traditional career paths to apply
red_flags: Remote role but excluded from CA, CT, NJ, NY, PA, WA — eliminates a large share of the remote-eligible US workforce
Compensation section separately excludes CA, CT, NJ, NY, PA, WA from being hired at all, yet also provides NY/NJ/WA/CA pay ranges — creates apparent contradiction
Most 'Desired' qualifications (partner onboarding, SI/ISV ecosystem, CRM tools) are core to the role and may functionally be required
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and comp)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and partner types)
ambiguities: The role says 'not eligible to be hired in CA, CT, NJ, NY, PA, WA' yet the compensation section provides pay ranges for CA, NY, NJ, and WA — likely a templated comp disclosure for legal compliance but the hiring exclusion takes precedence; both readings recorded.
The base_range_usd is set to the full spread across all disclosed geographies ($116,960 lowest to $171,900 highest); actual range for a given candidate depends on location.

Several 'Desired' qualifications (e.g., partner onboarding expertise, SI/ISV familiarity) are central to the role description and could be interpreted as functionally required; treated as soft per JD language.
